name: FC-72
review: CORRECTED: 3M datasheet gives 0.64 cP / 0.38 cSt (values were swapped/scaled). Resistivity is 1.0E15 ohm-cm = 1E13 ohm-m (unit mix-up). Breakdown ~38 kV (0.1 in gap) OK.

name: Transformer oil
review: Values plausible. Vol. heat capacity now formula-driven (875 x 1.9 = 1.66, was 1.70). Viscosity varies by grade (~9-20 cSt at 25 C).

name: Mineral oil
review: Vol. heat capacity was inconsistent: 850 x 1.85 = 1.57, not 1.65. Now formula-driven.

name: CFX70
review: Could not verify against a public datasheet - confirm with vendor TDS.

name: AC-100 by Ampcool
review: Vendor-specific (Engineered Fluids AmpCool) - verify against current TDS; viscosity/flash values not confirmed.

name: AC-110
review: Vendor-specific (Engineered Fluids AmpCool) - verify against current TDS; viscosity/flash values not confirmed.

name: AC-120
review: Vendor-specific (Engineered Fluids AmpCool) - verify against current TDS; viscosity/flash values not confirmed.

name: AC-130
review: Vendor-specific (Engineered Fluids AmpCool) - verify against current TDS; viscosity/flash values not confirmed.

name: AC-140
review: Vendor-specific (Engineered Fluids AmpCool) - verify against current TDS; viscosity/flash values not confirmed.

name: E5-TM410
review: Could not verify against a public datasheet - confirm with vendor TDS.

name: Water-glycol
review: Broadly OK for ~50/50 EG-water; density at 25 C is closer to 1070 kg/m3 than 1050. tan-delta=1 understood as placeholder for conductive.

name: Deionized water
notes: Excellent thermal fluid but conductivity rises with contamination
review: CORRECTED: theoretical max for ultrapure water is 18.2 Mohm-cm = 1.8E5 ohm-m; 1E6 ohm-m exceeds the physical limit. Practical loop values are lower and fall with contamination.

name: Pentaerythritol ester
review: Plausible generic values; confirm specific product grade.

name: HFO-1336 (SF33)
review: DUPLICATE: HFO-1336mzz(Z) IS Opteon SF33 - same fluid as SF33 row. CORRECTED per Chemours bulletin: k=0.077, rho~1360, 0.38 cP / 0.28 cSt, resistivity 1E8 ohm-cm = 1E6 ohm-m (polar fluid, NOT 1E15), breakdown 10 kV, BP 33.4 C. tan-delta not published; expected higher than PFCs (dielectric constant ~32).

name: C6F-ketone
review: Duplicate of Novec 649 row (same C6 fluoroketone) - same corrections applied. Consider merging.

name: F7A
review: Could not verify product identity/datasheet - confirm with vendor.

name: Novec 7000
review: Thermal/viscosity values OK vs 3M datasheet. CORRECTED resistivity: 3M lists 1E8 ohm-cm = 1E6 ohm-m, not 1E15. Verify breakdown voltage against 3M TDS (test-method dependent).

name: Novec 7100 (HFE-7100)
review: Same product as HFE-7100 row (methoxy-nonafluorobutane) - duplicate. Same corrections applied.

name: Novec 649
review: CORRECTED per 3M: thermal conductivity 0.059 (not 0.065), viscosity 0.64 cP / 0.40 cSt (not 1.4/0.9). Volume resistivity ~1E12 ohm-cm = 1E10 ohm-m (not 1E15). Breakdown ~40 kV OK.

name: MIVOLT DF7
review: Vendor ester (M&I Materials); density typically ~960-970 kg/m3 rather than 900 - verify against TDS.

name: SF49
review: Could not verify "SF49" as a product; values mirror Novec 649 (BP 49 C). Possibly conflated - confirm intended fluid (e.g., Chemours Opteon 2P50 boils at ~49 C).

name: Dimethyl silicone
review: INCONSISTENT: flash point (315) cannot exceed boiling point (300). Silicone oils have no true atmospheric BP - they decompose; BP set to ">250 (decomposes)". Flash ~280-320 applies to higher-viscosity grades.

name: OW-20
review: If this means 0W-20 engine oil, kinematic viscosity at 25 C is ~50 cSt (not 20). Verify intended product.

name: PAO
review: Plausible for PAO 4-6 grade; viscosity/flash vary strongly by grade.

name: Ethyl silicone oil
review: INCONSISTENT: flash (290) > listed BP (250) is physically impossible. BP set to ">250 (decomposes)". Verify grade-specific data.
